failure_story: A housing slowdown reduces settlements and margins while cancellations and deposit impairments rise; cash thought to be surplus funds deposits and mortgage working capital, and buybacks compound the loss.
earliest_warning_signals: Operating cash flow trails earnings after inventory normalization
Buybacks continue above defensible intrinsic value
forensic_checks: Reconcile cash flow to earnings and inventory/deposit movements
Track deposit allowance and impairment by controlled-lot growth
Compare repurchase price with contemporaneous evidence-supported value
unresolved_items: No community-level cycle history reproduces the owner-earnings range.
No lot-level exercise/opening schedule supports incremental option value without overlap.
No disclosed stress-liquidity policy proves the surplus-cash range.
